Checklist

Los Angeles permit record checklist.

Use this checklist before buying, listing, bidding, quoting, or spending money on plans. The goal is to catch the obvious record questions first.

1. Jurisdiction

  • City of Los Angeles, LA County, or another city?
  • Correct public portal?
  • Any assessor or parcel clues needed?

2. Address match

  • Exact street address
  • Unit or suite number
  • Old address format
  • Parcel or legal description clues

3. Permit timeline

  • Application date
  • Issue date
  • Revision dates
  • Inspection dates
  • Final, expiration, void, or withdrawal signals

4. Approved scope

Compare the public work description to the real-world claim. Remodel, addition, ADU, conversion, roof, electrical, plumbing, and structural work can carry different implications.

5. Red flags

  • Missing final
  • Expired permit
  • Scope mismatch
  • Wrong property
  • Open correction notes
  • Related trade permits missing

6. Decision point

If the record affects a deal, bid, listing, or design spend, do not stop at a portal screenshot. Get the next questions in writing.

PermitPulse next step

Need the checklist applied to one address? Order Permit Review Plus for $149, see the sample report, or send a free Snapshot.

FAQ

How long does a first permit check take?

A basic self-check may take minutes. A careful review takes longer because address, status, scope, and related records need to be compared.

Can a checklist prove approval?

No. It helps identify what to verify. Approval and compliance questions belong with the agency and qualified professionals.

What does Permit Review Plus include?

One address, official record screen, red flags, and a practical next-step memo.

Permit Problem Hotline

Text one address. We'll point you toward the records worth checking.
